title: Celebrating Everyone's Unique Story
story: October 26 is Intersex Awareness Day, when people around the world learn about and celebrate intersex individuals. Intersex people are born with bodies that don't fit typical definitions of male or female. On this day, communities hold educational events, share stories online, and display purple and yellow flags to show support. Schools might teach about body diversity, while families have conversations about how everyone's body is different and special in its own way.
activity_question: What colors are displayed on Intersex Awareness Day to show support?
follow_up_question: How can you show kindness to someone who looks or seems different from you?

title: Pumpkin Power Takes Over October!
story: Orange pumpkins rule the day on October 26 when National Pumpkin Day celebrates America's favorite fall fruit! People carve jack-o'-lanterns with spooky faces, bake pumpkin pies that smell amazing, and sip warm pumpkin spice lattes. Farmers show off their biggest pumpkins at contests, with some weighing over 1,000 pounds. Kids visit pumpkin patches to pick the perfect one for Halloween. Social media fills with pumpkin recipes, from roasted seeds to pumpkin bread. It's the ultimate day to go totally pumpkin crazy!
activity_question: What do people often carve pumpkins into on National Pumpkin Day?
bonus_fact: The heaviest pumpkin ever grown weighed over 2,700 pounds!

title: Mules Get Their Day to Shine
story: Every October 26, people across America celebrate National Mule Day to honor these amazing animals! Mules are the offspring of horses and donkeys, and they're incredibly strong and smart. On this special day, mule owners show off their animals at parades and festivals. Some people visit farms to learn about mules and even take rides. Others share photos and fun facts about mules on social media. Many folks discover that mules are actually more sure-footed than horses and can carry heavy loads up steep mountains. It's the perfect day to appreciate these hardworking, stubborn, and surprisingly gentle creatures!
activity_question: What makes mules special compared to horses?
activity_choices: They're more sure-footed and can carry heavy loads
activity_answer: They're more sure-footed and can carry heavy loads
bonus_fact: A mule's ears are much longer than a horse's ears!
